DTR  |  Listen to a Classroom Conversation
LCC-HS9-L1-1400P-HSSCIENG910-MEC-LAT-01

The Shape of the Fall

Click  🎧  to listen.
Listen to a conversation between a girl and a boy. Listen carefully. You will hear the conversation only once. After listening, you will answer some questions.
Listen to the Conversation
1 QUESTION 1: What are the students mainly trying to do?
2 QUESTION 2: Why does the boy want to explain orbital motion around the idea of balance?
3 QUESTION 3: What does the girl’s question about orbit shape tell us about how she is thinking?
Choose the best answer for each question. You may change your answers before submitting.
Answer all 3 questions to submit.
SMART SUPPORT:
Go back to the conversation. Locate the boy’s explanation of what produces the curved path, the girl’s restatement that identifies the two controlling factors, and the boy’s confirmation that connects those factors to the goal of the exhibit label. Note how the three pieces build on each other.
CA ELD PI.A.1, PI.A.3, PI.B.5  |  HS-ESS1-4  |  HS Science: Engineering Design 9–10
